era: 1970-present. Classic/Pop arc. Mushroom Records through Epic Records. The hits era: Magic Man, Barracuda, These Dreams, Alone, What About Love.
associated_musicians: Led Zeppelin (Ann Wilson influence: Robert Plant)

theory_nodes.concept: Engine's most important female-fronted band. Heart breaks hard rock's gender barrier through musicianship, not exception-making.
theory_nodes.description: Heart's cultural significance in rock history.

theory_nodes.concept: Heart PAIR: degrees 234 and 235 document two distinct eras. 234 = Classic/Pop (E=6). 235 = Hard Rock origins (E=7). The two eras are genuinely distinct.
theory_nodes.description: The two-degree Heart arc: commercial success vs. founding hard rock identity.

theory_nodes.concept: Mushroom Records incident produces Barracuda. Authentic anger makes great rock. The exploitation of the Wilson sisters' gender as marketing fuel = one of rock's great protest songs.

cross_references.relationship: E=6 continues. U-District psychedelia to Bellevue classic rock. Same energy band.
Same band, E=7. The founding identity. Crazy on You, Barracuda, Magic Man. One degree, one energy step.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=5 kestrel + E=6 Heart = E=11. FINAL FALCONIDAE. Smallest falcon meets classic rock's commercial peak. Both project further than their apparent scale.

milestones: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame 2013
